Title: Waxy Phenotype Evolution in the Allotetraploid Cereal Broomcorn Millet: Mutations at the GBSSI Locus in Their Functional and Phylogenetic Context

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Amylose synthesis in endosperm and pollen starch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from ADP-glucose to elongate essentially linear alpha-1,4-glucan chains in starch granules, producing amylose
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: ADP-glucose; growing alpha-1,4-glucan primer within starch granule
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines grain starch texture and physicochemical properties relevant to culinary use; lower amylose increases stickiness/glutinous texture
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm starch granules; pollen starch granules
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Terminal polymerization step in amylose formation within starch granules
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as the major determinant of endosperm amylose content in this species
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Active in endosperm and pollen based on phenotype/protein/activity analyses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Active S0 allele is sufficient for near wild-type endosperm amylose; mutant S-15 allele is essential for fully waxy phenotype when combined with nonfunctional GBSSI-L allele
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The GBSSI-S locus is the major locus controlling endosperm amylose content in Panicum miliaceum. 2. Genotypes carrying the active S0 allele showed approximately 35-40% amylose even when GBSSI-L was nonfunctional. 3. The S-15 loss-of-function allele strongly reduced amylose and was required for fully waxy endosperm together with mutant L alleles. 4. GBSSI-S also contributes to pollen amylose synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genotyping; sequencing; amylose assays; starch swelling power; GBSSI protein quantification; enzyme activity assay; iodine staining microscopy
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing or selecting GBSSI-S is the most effective route to alter broomcorn millet grain amylose and glutinous texture; S-15-like loss-of-function is a key target for generating waxy types, but intermediate combinations with active GBSSI-L may produce partial waxiness
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Partial waxy genotype showed intermediate amylose and swelling power and appears absent from landraces, suggesting possible negative selection against intermediate texture states
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The focal functional alleles discussed are S0 (active) and S-15 (15-bp deletion; loss of function). The study supports partial diploidization at the GBSSI locus, with GBSSI-S dominating grain amylose control.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microsatellite genotyping of 16 SSR loci; GBSSI genotyping in 178 plants; supplementary table S2

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Amylose synthesis in endosperm and pollen starch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from ADP-glucose to elongate alpha-1,4-glucan chains for amylose synthesis in starch granules
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: ADP-glucose; growing alpha-1,4-glucan primer within starch granule
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines grain starch texture and functional properties such as swelling power
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm starch granules; pollen starch granules
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Terminal polymerization step in amylose formation within starch granules
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not the major control point; minor contributor relative to GBSSI-S in endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function detected in endosperm and pollen; contribution in endosperm is much weaker than GBSSI-S
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Active LC allele alone in an S-15 background produces a partially waxy phenotype with about 11% amylose, demonstrating residual amylose synthesis capacity; mutant LY and Lf alleles contribute to fully waxy phenotype when combined with S-15
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GBSSI-L is a functional homeolog but contributes only a minor share of endosperm amylose synthesis. 2. The active LC allele in an S-15 background produced approximately 11% amylose and intermediate swelling power, establishing a partially waxy phenotype. 3. GBSSI-L contributes relatively little GBSSI protein and activity compared with GBSSI-S. 4. Mutant LY and Lf alleles are loss-of-function variants associated with waxy phenotypes when GBSSI-S is also mutant.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Identification and sequencing of S-15/LC lines; amylose quantification; iodine staining of endosperm sections and pollen; GBSSI protein SDS-PAGE; starch synthase activity assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: GBSSI-L can be used to fine-tune amylose content, but manipulating it alone is insufficient for full waxiness in grain; stacking nonfunctional GBSSI-L with GBSSI-S loss-of-function is required for fully waxy types
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Active LC with mutant S-15 gives intermediate texture traits and such partially waxy types are absent from landraces, consistent with possible negative selection
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional alleles discussed are LC (active but weak), LY (cysteine-to-tyrosine mutant, nonfunctional), and Lf (frameshift/insertion mutant, nonfunctional). The study infers partial diploidization/silencing trend of the L homeolog for grain amylose control.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microsatellite genotyping of 16 SSR loci; GBSSI genotyping in 178 plants; supplementary table S2

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes amylose chain elongation from ADP-glucose within starch granules
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to normal nonwaxy starch phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Implied functional in grain endosperm from iodine staining
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with dark blue-black iodine staining and wild-type nonwaxy endosperm starch in P. capillare; used as functional ortholog reference for P. miliaceum GBSSI-L
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A single diploid GBSSI sequence was identified in P. capillare. 2. It is orthologous to P. miliaceum GBSSI-L based on strong sequence similarity. 3. The gene is associated with wild-type nonwaxy endosperm starch and served as a functional evolutionary reference for assessing the reduced activity of P. miliaceum LC.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PCR amplification; direct sequencing; comparative sequence alignment; iodine staining of endosperm starch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a comparative functional allele to understand homeolog degeneration or reduced activity in allotetraploid millet; may inform allele replacement or functional restoration strategies
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This gene is not manipulated in broomcorn millet metabolism directly but is experimentally characterized in Results as a functional ortholog reference in phylogenetic and functional context.
